headline: OpenAI and Anthropic both filed confidentially for IPOs this quarter as agentic and coding techniques cooled from their earlier peaks.
body: OpenAI filed confidentially for an IPO roughly a week after Anthropic did the same, an escalating public-market race between the two leading labs, while Sam Altman's Tools for Humanity reportedly cut staff over weak revenue. Across techniques the rotation weakened: prompt engineering, agent orchestration, multi-session workflows, and iterative AI layer evolution all softened, and AI-assisted coding, the PIV Loop, System Evolution, and AI agents lagged. The fresh energy moved into infrastructure and throughput methods, with resource analysis, throughput scaling, CPU offloading, and DeepSpeed Ulysses entering as new throughput scaling concerns, alongside breakouts in attribution and filter propagation. Apple drew steady attention through its WWDC Siri demos, a Core AI library for running PyTorch models on Apple hardware, and a cheaper-AI push aimed at smaller developers.

cited_items.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
